findings.description: Repeated page header/footer content in HTML
findings.evidence: The following text blocks repeat 3+ times, likely page headers/footers that should not appear in the body: 'process components' (15x); 'evaluation criteria' (7x); 'hydrologic model' (4x).

findings.description: Gear mechanism diagram (showing interlocking relationship between Basin Plan, Basin Model, and Water Resources Management) and concentric circles diagram (showing hierarchical nesting of Water Resources Management > Integrated Basin Plan > Hydrologic Model) are missing as visual elements. Only flat text labels remain, losing the relational meaning conveyed by the diagrams.
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 shows a gear diagram with three interlocking gears and a concentric circles diagram illustrating hierarchical relationships. HTML renders these as a flat list of text labels: 'Water Resources Management', 'Integrated Basin Plan', 'HYDROLOGIC MODEL', 'Basin Plan', 'Basin Model', 'Water Resources Management' without any visual diagram.

findings.description: Venn diagram showing project organization with three overlapping circles is missing. The overlap between circles conveys shared responsibilities/collaboration between teams, which is lost in the flat text rendering.
findings.evidence: PDF page 21 shows a Venn diagram with three overlapping circles: 'Data Coordination / HDR', 'Model Development/Data Process / HydroLogics Team / Hazen and Sawyer', and 'Data, Model Evaluation & Plan Development / NCDWR'. HTML renders these as separate text blocks without showing the overlapping visual relationship.

findings.description: Table 4-3 (Water Supply Demand & Deficits) appears both as a PDF image and as a separately rendered HTML table, creating duplicated content that interrupts reading flow.
findings.evidence: PDF page 18 shows one table. HTML shows the same table twice: once as the original PDF image of the table and once as a properly structured HTML table with identical data. Both versions appear sequentially in the reading flow.

findings.description: Water Supply Deficits table from PDF page 18 is rendered BOTH as an tag AND as a separate HTML element, duplicating the same content in two forms.
findings.evidence: HTML contains

findings.description: Tasks 2 and 4 from PDF page 22 each appear twice in the HTML — once as an and once as text.
findings.evidence: HTML has followed by Task 2 - OASIS Application Development, and similarly for Task 4. In the PDF each appears only once (as grayed-out text).

findings.description: Stream Condition bar chart from PDF page 17 is missing as an image. Only text descriptions of axes and legend items are provided.
findings.evidence: PDF page 17 shows a bar chart titled 'Stream Condition Middle Deep River (Node 280) June - November' with grouped bars showing % of Days at Flow Level across four demand scenarios. The HTML section has no tag for this chart — only text descriptions of Y-axis, X-axis categories, and legend items.

findings.description: Concentric circles diagram from PDF page 3 showing nested relationships (HYDROLOGIC MODEL → Integrated Basin Plan → Water Resources Management) is missing as an image.
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 shows concentric circles with HYDROLOGIC MODEL at center, Integrated Basin Plan in middle ring, and Water Resources Management as outer ring. HTML represents this as plain text paragraphs with no tag.

findings.description: HTML adds axis-label prefixes 'Y-axis:' and 'X-axis categories:' for the page 17 chart description that do not appear anywhere in the PDF.
findings.evidence:
HTML contains: '

Y-axis: % of Days at Flow Level (0.0% to 35.0%)

' and '

X-axis categories: Unimpaired, 2003 Demands, 2030 Demands, 2050 Demands

'. The PDF has '% of Days at Flow Level' as a chart axis label and the category names on the chart axis, but the 'Y-axis:' and 'X-axis categories:' prefixes are fabricated connective text.
